speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Master lieutenant, now that God and friends

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Have shaken Edward from the regal seat

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And turned my captive state to liberty,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My fear to hope, my sorrows unto joys,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: At our enlargement what are thy due fees?

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Subjects may challenge nothing of their sov’reigns,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: But, if an humble prayer may prevail,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: I then crave pardon of your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For what, lieutenant? For well using me?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nay, be thou sure I’ll well requite thy kindness,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For that it made my imprisonment a pleasure,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, such a pleasure as encagèd birds

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Conceive when, after many moody thoughts,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: At last by notes of household harmony

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They quite forget their loss of liberty.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But, Warwick, after God thou sett’st me free,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And chiefly, therefore, I thank God and thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He was the author, thou the instrument.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Therefore, that I may conquer Fortune’s spite

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: By living low where Fortune cannot hurt me,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And that the people of this blessèd land

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: May not be punished with my thwarting stars,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Warwick, although my head still wear the crown,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I here resign my government to thee,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For thou art fortunate in all thy deeds.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Your Grace hath still been famed for virtuous

speaker: WARWICK
text: And now may seem as wise as virtuous

speaker: WARWICK
text: By spying and avoiding Fortune’s malice,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For few men rightly temper with the stars.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Yet, in this one thing let me blame your Grace:

speaker: WARWICK
text: For choosing me when Clarence is in place.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: No, Warwick, thou art worthy of the sway,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To whom the heav’ns in thy nativity

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Adjudged an olive branch and laurel crown

speaker: CLARENCE
text: As likely to be blest in peace and war;

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And therefore I yield thee my free consent.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And I choose Clarence only for Protector.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Warwick and Clarence, give me both your hands.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now join your hands, and with your hands your hearts,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That no dissension hinder government.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I make you both Protectors of this land,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: While I myself will lead a private life

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And in devotion spend my latter days,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To sin’s rebuke and my Creator’s praise.

speaker: WARWICK
text: What answers Clarence to his sovereign’s will?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That he consents, if Warwick yield consent,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: For on thy fortune I repose myself.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why, then, though loath, yet must I be content.

speaker: WARWICK
text: We’ll yoke together like a double shadow

speaker: WARWICK
text: To Henry’s body, and supply his place—

speaker: WARWICK
text: I mean, in bearing weight of government—

speaker: WARWICK
text: While he enjoys the honor and his ease.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And, Clarence, now then it is more than needful

speaker: WARWICK
text: Forthwith that Edward be pronounced a traitor

speaker: WARWICK
text: And all his lands and goods be confiscate.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: What else? And that succession be determinèd.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Ay, therein Clarence shall not want his part.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But with the first of all your chief affairs

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let me entreat—for I command no more—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That Margaret your queen and my son Edward

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be sent for, to return from France with speed,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For till I see them here, by doubtful fear

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My joy of liberty is half eclipsed.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: It shall be done, my sovereign, with all speed.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My lord of Somerset, what youth is that

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Of whom you seem to have so tender care?

speaker: SOMERSET
text: My liege, it is young Henry, Earl of Richmond.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come hither, England’s hope.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If secret powers

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Suggest but truth to my divining thoughts,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This pretty lad will prove our country’s bliss.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His looks are full of peaceful majesty,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His head by nature framed to wear a crown,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His hand to wield a scepter, and himself

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Likely in time to bless a regal throne.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Make much of him, my lords, for this is he

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Must help you more than you are hurt by me.

speaker: WARWICK
text: What news, my friend?

speaker: POST
text: That Edward is escapèd from your brother

speaker: POST
text: And fled, as he hears since, to Burgundy.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unsavory news! But how made he escape?

speaker: POST
text: He was conveyed by Richard, Duke of Gloucester,

speaker: POST
text: And the Lord Hastings, who attended him

speaker: POST
text: In secret ambush on the forest side

speaker: POST
text: And from the Bishop’s huntsmen rescued him,

speaker: POST
text: For hunting was his daily exercise.

speaker: WARWICK
text: My brother was too careless of his charge.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But let us hence, my sovereign, to provide

speaker: WARWICK
text: A salve for any sore that may betide.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: My lord, I like not of this flight of Edward’s,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: For doubtless Burgundy will yield him help,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And we shall have more wars before ’t be long.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: As Henry’s late presaging prophecy

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Did glad my heart with hope of this young Richmond,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: So doth my heart misgive me in these conflicts

speaker: SOMERSET
text: What may befall him, to his harm and ours.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Therefore, Lord Oxford, to prevent the worst,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Forthwith we’ll send him hence to Brittany

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Till storms be past of civil enmity.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Ay, for if Edward repossess the crown,

speaker: OXFORD
text: ’Tis like that Richmond, with the rest, shall down.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: It shall be so. He shall to Brittany.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Come, therefore, let’s about it speedily.
